text: An iron-catalyzed protocol has been developed for the intermolecular 1,2-addition of perfluoroalkyl iodides to alkynes and alkenes. This methodology exhibits a broad substrate scope and excellent tolerance for diverse functional groups, enabling the efficient incorporation of various perfluoroalkyl sources, including the challenging gaseous reagent CF3I. The resulting perfluoroalkylated alkyl and alkenyl iodides serve as versatile intermediates that can be further elaborated through various cross-coupling transformations. By utilizing earth-abundant iron catalysis, this approach offers a straightforward, streamlined, and sustainable route to sophisticated perfluoroalkylated organic molecules.
